title: boykinia heucheriformis
intro: Boykinia heucheriformis, also known as the squirrel corn or eastern boykinia, is a perennial plant species native to eastern North America. It belongs to the saxifragaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, and to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Boykinia heucheriformis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
